affected_pattern: Active customer with InternetService=Fiber, MonthlyCharges suddenly 0
root_cause: Billing system failed to process CDR for current cycle. Likely cause: CDR ingestion pipeline timeout during batch processing window, resulting in zero-rated billing record.
supporting_evidence: 3GPP TS 32.240 Section 5.2 — CDR processing timeout handling
SLA Document — Section 3.1: Billing accuracy guarantee >= 99.5%
Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: CDR Ingestion Pipeline Timeout pattern
recommended_action: Trigger CDR reprocessing for affected account
Verify CDR pipeline health for batch window
Issue corrective billing statement
estimated_revenue_impact: MonthlyCharges x affected_accounts
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer with active PhoneService and multiple streaming services showing $0 monthly charge
root_cause: Provisioning system mismatch — customer was provisioned for services but billing system not updated. Service activation event did not propagate to billing platform.
supporting_evidence: Provisioning-to-billing sync protocol documentation
Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Provisioning System Mismatch pattern
CRM shows active services but billing system shows inactive
recommended_action: Resync provisioning system with billing platform
Issue corrective bill for missed billing cycles
Audit provisioning event queue for other stuck messages
estimated_revenue_impact: MonthlyCharges x missed_cycles
author: seed

affected_pattern: Multiple customers on same tariff plan showing zero charges in same billing cycle
root_cause: Rating engine configuration error — incorrect tariff plan configuration rating service usage at $0 for the specific plan variant.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Rating Engine Configuration Error pattern
Multiple customers on same plan affected simultaneously
Recent configuration change to tariff plan identified in change log
recommended_action: Correct tariff configuration in rating engine
Reprocess all affected CDRs for the billing cycle
Implement configuration change validation checks
estimated_revenue_impact: Sum of expected charges for all affected customers
author: seed

affected_pattern: Postpaid customers converted from prepaid in the last 30 days showing $0 MonthlyCharges
root_cause: Prepaid-to-postpaid conversion workflow failure — subscriber profile was migrated in the CRM but the recurring charge schedule was never activated in the billing catalog, leaving the account in a zero-recurring state.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Plan Conversion Workflow Gap pattern
Conversion timestamp in CRM precedes absence of recurring charge record in billing DB
Ops change ticket CHG-48213 for conversion workflow deployed 32 days ago
recommended_action: Re-trigger recurring charge activation for all conversions in the last 30 days
Back-bill customers from their conversion date per regulatory grace window
Add post-conversion validation step to the workflow DAG
File customer notification before back-billing to comply with consumer protection rules
estimated_revenue_impact: Cumulative missed recurring charges x affected conversions
author: seed

affected_pattern: Accounts with recently expired promotional bundles showing $0 on the bill after bundle end
root_cause: Promotion expiry handler did not re-activate the base tariff — the offer-management microservice marked the promotion inactive but failed to publish a 'restore-base-tariff' event, leaving the billing catalog without any active rate plan.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Promotion Expiry Handler Defect pattern
Kafka topic offer-events has 'promotion.ended' without matching 'tariff.restored' events
Offer-management service release 2026.03.11 changed event emission order
recommended_action: Publish retroactive 'tariff.restored' events for all affected accounts
Reprocess the current billing cycle to apply base tariff charges
Hotfix offer-management service to emit both events atomically
Add a reconciliation job comparing offer-ended vs tariff-restored counts daily
estimated_revenue_impact: Base-tariff amount x affected accounts x cycles
author: seed

affected_pattern: International-roaming-heavy customers show $0 international charges despite active roaming usage in CDRs
root_cause: Roaming partner rate-sheet not loaded for the current month — the inter-operator tariff file from the visited PLMN failed its signature-verification step and the rating engine silently fell back to a $0 rate for unrecognized operators.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Missing Roaming Rate Sheet pattern
Rating engine warning log: 'partner-tariff-not-found, defaulting to 0.0'
TAP 3.12 rate file ingestion job failed silently for partner MCC 262
recommended_action: Reload and verify roaming rate sheet for MCC 262
Rerate all roaming CDRs in the affected period
Fix rating engine to fail-closed (reject rather than $0) on missing partner rates
Add alerting when TAP file ingestion exits with non-zero status
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled international roaming revenue
author: seed

affected_pattern: Enterprise accounts under consolidated billing show $0 for one of several child accounts
root_cause: Hierarchy-resolver bug in consolidated billing — a parent account's charge-aggregation query skipped child accounts whose ledger had been touched by a pending credit note, leaving those children at $0 on the consolidated invoice.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Consolidated Billing Hierarchy Defect pattern
All affected child accounts have an open credit_note row with status=pending
SQL in consolidated-invoice job filters out rows where credit_note_pending IS NOT NULL
recommended_action: Regenerate consolidated invoices including the skipped children
Issue supplementary invoice for the missed charges
Fix the aggregation SQL to include pending credit notes as separate line items
Add a regression test covering parent-child with pending credits
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled child-account charges
author: seed

affected_pattern: Recently activated accounts all show $0 for their first full billing cycle
root_cause: First-bill proration defect — a flag meant to zero the pro-rata partial cycle was incorrectly applied to the first full cycle as well, leading to $0 bills for newly onboarded accounts.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: First-Bill Proration Flag Misapplied pattern
Proration-flag code path introduced in release billing-engine 4.7
All affected customers have activation_date within last 35 days
recommended_action: Recompute first full cycle charges for all newly onboarded accounts
Issue corrective invoice with clear customer communication
Patch billing-engine to apply proration flag only to partial-cycle charges
Add unit test covering partial vs full-cycle proration branches
estimated_revenue_impact: First-month revenue x affected new customers
author: seed

affected_pattern: Accounts whose tenure just crossed a loyalty-tier threshold show zero recurring charges
root_cause: Loyalty-tier discount misconfiguration — a newly introduced 'platinum' tier discount was configured as 100% (intended 10%), applying a full waiver instead of a partial credit to all customers who just qualified.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Discount Config Off-By-Factor pattern
Discount row loyalty_tier=platinum has value=1.00 in the pricing table
Change ticket CHG-51204 deployed the tier two weeks ago
recommended_action: Correct the discount value from 1.00 to 0.10 in the pricing table
Rerate affected cycles and issue differential invoices
Add a schema constraint CHECK (discount_value BETWEEN 0 AND 0.99)
Require two-person review for pricing-table changes going forward
estimated_revenue_impact: 90% of recurring revenue for affected platinum-tier customers
author: seed

affected_pattern: All customers invoiced between 02:00 and 03:00 UTC on DST spring-forward day show $0
root_cause: Daylight-saving-time transition defect — the billing engine's cycle-window query used local time without DST awareness, producing a zero-duration window during the skipped hour and generating empty invoices.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: DST Transition Empty Window pattern
Invoicing job logs show cycle_window_seconds=0 for the affected batch
Known issue with jdbc driver timezone handling (US/Eastern)
recommended_action: Rerun invoicing job for the affected hour using UTC-normalized windows
Void the empty invoices and reissue correct ones
Migrate all billing windows to UTC with explicit timezone conversion at presentation
Add a regression test exercising spring-forward and fall-back days
estimated_revenue_impact: Single-hour cohort revenue, small but reputationally visible
author: seed

affected_pattern: Specific customer segment (family plan primary lines) shows $0 while secondary lines are billed normally
root_cause: Family-plan splitter attributed the full plan charge to secondary lines and nothing to the primary — a recent refactor inverted the primary/secondary flag when computing the per-line allocation.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Multi-Line Allocation Inversion pattern
Secondary lines show duplicated full charges (matching Duplicate-Charge pattern)
Commit 7a3f2bc in family-plan-splitter flipped the is_primary boolean
recommended_action: Revert commit 7a3f2bc and redeploy the splitter
Rerate the affected billing cycle and rebalance charges across lines
Issue adjustment invoices per affected family account
Add an invariant test: sum(allocations) == plan_total AND primary_share > 0
estimated_revenue_impact: Zero primary revenue on affected family accounts
author: seed

affected_pattern: Accounts whose certificate-based inter-service auth just expired show $0
root_cause: Expired mTLS certificate between billing and provisioning services — rate-lookup calls began failing silently (caught exception → default=0.0) after the cert expired at midnight UTC, so rating defaulted to zero for all downstream lookups.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Inter-Service Auth Failure pattern
Expired cert CN=billing-to-provisioning with NotAfter matching failure window
Exception log 'SSLHandshakeException' with swallowed stack trace in RateLookupClient
recommended_action: Renew and rotate the inter-service certificate
Restart rating-engine instances to pick up the new cert
Replace silent exception catch with fail-closed behavior and explicit alert
Onboard the cert to the auto-renewal pipeline with 30-day expiry alerting
estimated_revenue_impact: All recurring + usage revenue for the outage window
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer billed twice for same monthly charges with identical timestamps
root_cause: CDR deduplication engine failure — the deduplication process was bypassed during a system restart, allowing duplicate CDR records to pass through to the rating engine.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: CDR Deduplication Engine Failure pattern
3GPP TS 32.240 Section 4.3 — CDR uniqueness requirements
System restart event logged 30 minutes before duplicate CDRs appeared
recommended_action: Remove duplicate CDRs from billing records
Issue refund/credit for overcharged amount
Restart and validate deduplication engine
Reprocess affected billing records
estimated_revenue_impact: Duplicate charge amount per affected customer
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer shows doubled MonthlyCharges with two billing entries separated by seconds
root_cause: Billing system retry logic error — the retry mechanism for a failed transaction duplicated the charge when the original transaction actually succeeded but confirmation was lost.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Billing System Retry Logic Error pattern
Two charge entries with same amount but different transaction IDs
System timeout event logged between the two transactions
recommended_action: Implement transaction-level idempotency checks
Reverse the duplicate charge
Add unique transaction ID verification before charge posting
Audit retry logic configuration
estimated_revenue_impact: MonthlyCharges x duplicate_count
author: seed

affected_pattern: Enterprise customer with bundled services billed by two different billing subsystems
root_cause: Cross-system synchronization failure — voice and data billing systems both processed the same usage events for a bundled service plan, resulting in double billing.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Cross-System Synchronization Failure pattern
Charges from two different billing system IDs for same usage
Recent change to billing system orchestration routing rules
recommended_action: Fix routing rules to prevent cross-system duplicate processing
Reconcile and reverse duplicate charges
Strengthen inter-system deduplication checks
estimated_revenue_impact: Total duplicate amount across affected accounts
author: seed

affected_pattern: Charges spike exactly 2x for a contiguous window of accounts processed after a Kafka consumer rebalance
root_cause: Kafka consumer group rebalance replay — the rating consumer's offset commit was asynchronous and the rebalance replayed ~5 minutes of CDRs, causing the same events to be charged twice.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Kafka Replay on Rebalance pattern
Consumer lag chart shows spike and replay at rebalance timestamp
enable.auto.commit=true and commit interval exceeded processing latency
recommended_action: Switch rating consumer to synchronous commit after successful rating
Introduce idempotency key (CDR ID + billing_cycle) at the charge-insert step
Issue reversals for the duplicate charge window
Add consumer-lag-spike alert as an early signal
estimated_revenue_impact: 2x rated revenue for the replay window
author: seed

affected_pattern: Charges double only for accounts whose region failed over to the DR site last night
root_cause: DR failback replay — the primary site came back online without a 'charges-already-posted' reconciliation, and the primary replayed the DR-processed queue, creating duplicates.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: DR Failback Replay pattern
Failover timestamp aligns with start of duplicate window
No reconciliation job recorded in the runbook execution log for failback
recommended_action: Reverse duplicates identified by (account_id, cdr_hash) present on both sites
Add mandatory reconciliation step to the failback runbook
Persist a global 'posted-set' in the shared cache referenced by both sites
Tabletop exercise for DR cutover covering idempotency
estimated_revenue_impact: Duplicated revenue for DR-served traffic
author: seed

affected_pattern: A reversal posted but the original charge was not voided, leading to a net-neutral ledger but a doubled invoice amount
root_cause: Refund-without-void accounting bug — the CSR-initiated refund created an opposing entry rather than voiding the original, which the invoice-generator summed as two separate positive charges in the 'charges' column while netting to zero in the ledger.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Refund-Without-Void pattern
Invoice line items show original + reversal as two positive entries
Accounting ledger nets to zero but customer-visible invoice does not
recommended_action: Void the original charge and remove the opposing reversal from invoice view
Regenerate and resend affected invoices with apology communication
Change refund workflow to issue a void for same-cycle cancellations
Add invoice invariant: no two same-amount charges within 60 seconds
estimated_revenue_impact: Reputational; ledger impact zero but customer complaints expected
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customers in multi-state regions see same usage charged twice under different jurisdictional tax lines
root_cause: Tax engine double-levy — after the rollout of the new location-resolver, cross-state customers matched both origin and destination tax jurisdictions and the tax engine applied both levies instead of choosing the canonical one.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Cross-Jurisdiction Double Tax pattern
Tax engine release notes for location-resolver v2 list this as a known edge case
Affected accounts all have origin_state != destination_state
recommended_action: Revert location-resolver to v1 or hotfix the jurisdiction-selection logic
Issue tax credit for the duplicate jurisdictional charges
Regulatory disclosure if required in the affected states
Add a jurisdiction-conflict test matrix covering 10 multi-state scenarios
estimated_revenue_impact: Extra tax line amount across affected accounts plus regulatory risk
author: seed

affected_pattern: Subscription charges appear once under 'recurring' and once under 'daily' on the same invoice
root_cause: Daily and monthly schedulers both activated for the same service — a migration from daily-prorated to monthly-fixed billing left the daily job enabled in production, emitting charges on both schedules.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Dual-Scheduler Migration Gap pattern
Cron table shows both monthly_billing.sh and daily_billing.sh enabled for the affected plan
Migration runbook step 'disable daily scheduler' marked incomplete
recommended_action: Disable the daily scheduler for migrated plans immediately
Reverse the daily-component charges on affected invoices
Add a runbook checklist gate in the deployment system
Emit a billing-scheduler metric and alert when > 1 scheduler per plan is active
estimated_revenue_impact: 31x intended rate (daily + monthly) for the migration window
author: seed

affected_pattern: Dual-homed CDR collection produced duplicate records in Africa-region accounts
root_cause: Dual-homed CDR forwarding — both primary and standby mediation paths were active simultaneously after a regional network change, and the CDR router forwarded to both, doubling the events delivered to rating.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Dual-Homed Mediation pattern
Network change NC-8172 set primary=active AND standby=active on CDR router
Rating input topic message count exactly 2x the network element emission count
recommended_action: Reset CDR router to active/standby mode (one active path only)
Deduplicate rating input for the overlap window by (IMSI, timestamp, cdr_id)
Reverse duplicated charges on affected customer invoices
Add a continuous check on CDR source-to-rating count ratio; alert if > 1.05
estimated_revenue_impact: Doubled usage revenue in the affected region for the window
author: seed

affected_pattern: Enterprise customers with long-running sessions see intermediate and final CDRs both rated as full sessions
root_cause: Interim CDR semantics lost in new rating pipeline — the refactored pipeline stopped correlating interim (S-CDR) and final (F-CDR) CDRs by session-ID, treating each as a standalone billable session instead of aggregating.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Interim/Final CDR Correlation Defect pattern
3GPP TS 32.298 section on S-CDR and F-CDR semantics cites this as a known hazard
Pipeline rewrite removed the session-ID correlation index
recommended_action: Re-introduce session-ID correlation and aggregation in the rating pipeline
Re-rate long sessions in the affected window and adjust charges
Issue credit for double-counted session minutes
Contract test the pipeline against a synthetic session spanning 3 interim and 1 final CDR
estimated_revenue_impact: Session-charge doubling for affected enterprise customers
author: seed

affected_pattern: Recurring charges posted on both the 1st and the 2nd of the month for a subset of accounts
root_cause: Scheduler retry without idempotency — the invoicing job failed at 23:59 on the 1st, was restarted at 00:01 on the 2nd by the watchdog, and both runs posted recurring charges since the idempotency key was derived from the run timestamp.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Scheduler Retry Idempotency Defect pattern
Watchdog log shows job restart two minutes after failure
Idempotency key uses wall-clock date rather than logical billing date
recommended_action: Reverse the duplicate postings on the 2nd
Change idempotency key to (account_id, billing_cycle_id) deterministically
Make the invoicing job a pure function of inputs to allow safe retry
Add a post-run assertion that charge count == expected account count
estimated_revenue_impact: Duplicate monthly recurring revenue for affected accounts
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer reports seeing the same $ amount posted at the same timestamp on two line items under different SKUs
root_cause: Catalog SKU duplication — a catalog refresh introduced a shadow SKU that pointed to the same rating rule, causing the product catalog join to emit two rows per service, each charged separately.
supporting_evidence: Duplicate Charge RCA Playbook: Catalog Shadow-SKU pattern
Catalog table shows two SKUs with identical rating_rule_id
Catalog refresh ticket CAT-2031 introduced the shadow SKU 14 days ago
recommended_action: Deactivate the shadow SKU in the catalog
Reverse the duplicate SKU charges and consolidate to a single line item
Add a unique constraint (rating_rule_id) on the SKU table
Automate catalog refresh diff-review before publishing to production
estimated_revenue_impact: Per-SKU doubling; moderate
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer data usage increased 10x overnight with charges far above historical baseline
root_cause: Potential fraudulent SIM cloning or account compromise — unauthorized usage detected from geographic locations inconsistent with customer's profile.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Fraudulent SIM Cloning pattern
Usage from two different geographic locations simultaneously
Customer profile shows no travel history
recommended_action: Immediately suspend account for security investigation
Issue replacement SIM to customer
Reverse fraudulent charges
File fraud report with security team
estimated_revenue_impact: Fraudulent usage charges requiring reversal
author: seed

affected_pattern: Monthly charges jumped to 10x normal but usage pattern shows continuous 24/7 data consumption
root_cause: Device malware or malfunctioning application generating excessive background data usage. Customer's device has a rogue process consuming data continuously.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Device Malware pattern
Continuous 24/7 usage pattern (not human-like burst pattern)
Data usage is upload-heavy (malware exfiltration indicator)
recommended_action: Advise customer on device security scan
Apply retroactive data cap if plan allows
Consider goodwill credit for device-related issue
Flag for customer outreach
estimated_revenue_impact: Potential goodwill credit amount
author: seed

affected_pattern: Multiple customers on same plan showing exactly 10x usage spike simultaneously
root_cause: Rating engine metering error — a bug in the rating engine multiplied usage quantities by an incorrect factor (10x) for all customers on the affected plan.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Metering/Rating Error pattern
Multiple customers on same plan affected simultaneously
Spike is exactly 10x normal (round multiplier)
Recent rating engine configuration change identified
recommended_action: Correct rating engine calculation logic
Reprocess all affected CDRs with correct metering
Issue corrective billing for all affected customers
Implement rating accuracy regression tests
estimated_revenue_impact: Overcharged amount across all affected customers
author: seed

affected_pattern: IoT-tier accounts show a sudden sustained 10x data usage during off-peak hours
root_cause: Runaway IoT device firmware loop — a firmware update pushed to a class of connected sensors introduced a retry loop that continuously re-established data sessions, each generating a full handshake plus payload.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Runaway IoT Firmware pattern
All affected devices share the same firmware version deployed last week
CDR duration distribution bimodal: short handshakes repeated at millisecond interval
recommended_action: Coordinate with IoT partner to roll back the firmware
Apply emergency data cap on the affected device SKUs
Credit the excess usage under IoT-partner SLA
Add a pre-deployment traffic shaping test for firmware updates
estimated_revenue_impact: Largely refund/credit; partner-covered under SLA
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer usage 10x normal correlates with a video streaming app update date
root_cause: Streaming app default changed to ultra-HD — a popular streaming app pushed an update that defaulted to 4K streaming on cellular, multiplying data usage for all its users on the mobile network.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Streaming-App Default Bitrate Change pattern
User-agent on high-usage sessions matches the app update version
Public release notes confirm the default-4K change
recommended_action: Customer-facing advisory with instructions to change app settings
Offer one-time goodwill credit and a data-booster option
Engage the streaming partner about lowering cellular default bitrate
Add traffic-classification telemetry so future partner-driven shifts are detected faster
estimated_revenue_impact: Goodwill credits; partially recoverable via boosters
author: seed

affected_pattern: Handover-heavy customers in high-mobility routes show 10x session counts
root_cause: LTE-to-5G inter-RAT handover double-counting — the mediation layer counted both the LTE closeout CDR and the 5G startup CDR as separate billable sessions instead of stitching them, inflating session and volume counts.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Handover Session-Stitching Defect pattern
3GPP TS 32.298 section on session continuity flags referenced in engineering ticket
Spike magnitude correlates with customer mobility score from RAN analytics
recommended_action: Re-enable the session-continuity flag in the mediation config
Re-rate affected sessions by stitching paired CDRs
Issue credits for the over-counted sessions
Add a cross-RAT continuity counter to the dashboard
estimated_revenue_impact: Overcharged session volume for mobile-heavy users
author: seed

affected_pattern: VPN-enabled business accounts show 10x data usage after a corporate client config push
root_cause: VPN full-tunnel misconfiguration — a corporate MDM push set the VPN client to full-tunnel on cellular instead of split-tunnel, so all device traffic (including streaming and backup) now counts against the cellular plan.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: VPN Routing Misconfig pattern
Traffic mix shifted from previously application-scoped to all-HTTPS to one egress
Corporate client change ticket CORP-9912 rolled out the same day
recommended_action: Work with the corporate customer to revert to split-tunnel
Offer temporary data-cap relief while the rollback completes
Credit excess usage proportionally to the mis-configuration window
Add detection rule: sustained > 5x historical baseline + egress fan-in
estimated_revenue_impact: Partial credit + revenue retention
author: seed

affected_pattern: Volumetric spike isolated to uplink while downlink stays flat
root_cause: DDoS amplification exposure — the customer's IoT gateway was exploited as a reflector in a DNS amplification attack, sending large uplink packets in response to small queries.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Amplification-Reflection Exploit pattern
Uplink-to-downlink ratio inverted from historical norm
Threat intel feed flagged the destination IP as an amplification target
recommended_action: Block amplification-source ports at the PGW/UPF for the affected APN
Credit the fraudulent uplink traffic
Advise the customer to patch the gateway firmware
Add an uplink-skew anomaly detector to the security pipeline
estimated_revenue_impact: Credited uplink usage + security remediation
author: seed

affected_pattern: High-tier 5G customers show 10x volumetric usage same day as network capacity upgrade
root_cause: 5G speed uplift without plan uplift — a RAN software upgrade increased peak throughput, and customers on flat-rate-with-soft-cap plans consumed proportionally more, pushing them past cap into premium rates.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Speed-Uplift-Without-Plan-Alignment pattern
RAN config note confirms peak throughput cap raised from 200 to 1000 Mbps
Spike magnitude correlates with customer device 5G capability
recommended_action: Retroactively raise the soft cap for the upgrade month
Offer cap-upgrade migration path to affected customers
Align network-upgrade and plan-upgrade change management going forward
Add a pre-upgrade billing-impact assessment to RAN change process
estimated_revenue_impact: Partial credit; long-term ARPU uplift possible
author: seed

affected_pattern: Small cohort of customers on metered tethering show 10x usage with no change in primary-device usage
root_cause: Tethering cap bypass defect — a UE OS update allowed tethered traffic to be tagged as primary-device traffic in the APN header, bypassing the tethering-metered rate that applied the cap.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Tethering-Tag Bypass pattern
Device OS version common across affected customers
Deep-packet inspection confirms tethered flows lacking the tethering-tag
recommended_action: Deploy PGW rule to reclassify flows by TTL/user-agent heuristics
Issue partial credit for mis-classified overage
Engage device OEM regarding the OS behavior change
Add metric: tethering-tag presence per APN; alert on drop
estimated_revenue_impact: Partially recoverable overage revenue
author: seed

affected_pattern: Dormant accounts suddenly appearing with 10x usage in a distant country
root_cause: Account takeover via SIM swap — adversary performed SIM swap through a social-engineered CSR and immediately initiated high-value international calling and data.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: SIM Swap Account Takeover pattern
CSR audit trail shows SIM swap minutes before first fraudulent CDR
Destination numbers match known high-risk international ranges
recommended_action: Immediate suspension and fraud investigation for affected accounts
Reverse all fraudulent charges per operator fraud policy
Require callback + 2FA for any future SIM swap
Retrain CSR staff on social-engineering red flags
estimated_revenue_impact: Full write-off + regulatory complaint risk
author: seed

affected_pattern: A cohort of customers shows 10x usage every first-hour-of-day for one week
root_cause: Daily reset window double-counting — after a metering service restart, the daily-counter reset job ran at both 00:00 and 00:05 UTC for a week, flushing the daily accumulator twice and shifting the excess into the paid bucket at the hour boundary.
supporting_evidence: Usage Spike RCA Playbook: Daily-Counter Reset Defect pattern
Reset-job log shows duplicate execution across 7 days
Spike confined to the 00:00–01:00 hour consistently
recommended_action: Remove the duplicate reset job and restore single 00:00 UTC schedule
Re-rate the week and refund the misattributed paid-bucket usage
Add a reset-job ledger so double executions are visible as metrics
Add a daily invariant test: accumulator resets exactly once per day
estimated_revenue_impact: Refund of paid-bucket usage for the week
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer billing record has NULL TotalCharges indicating CDR processing failure
root_cause: CDR format mismatch after network element upgrade — the CDR format changed but the mediation system was not updated, causing field parsing failures and NULL values in billing records.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Format Mismatch After Network Upgrade pattern
Network upgrade event logged 24 hours before failures started
CDR rejection logs show format-specific parsing errors
recommended_action: Update mediation device CDR parsing rules for new format
Reprocess all rejected CDRs since the network upgrade
Validate end-to-end CDR processing after fix
Add format validation to pre-upgrade checklist
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled usage for all affected accounts
author: seed

affected_pattern: Batch of customer records with missing TotalCharges during peak traffic period
root_cause: Data pipeline overflow during peak traffic — CDR processing pipeline buffers overflowed during peak hours, causing CDRs to be dropped and resulting in NULL billing fields.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Data Pipeline Overflow pattern
Failures correlate with peak traffic hours (18:00-22:00)
Pipeline throughput metrics show capacity exceeded 120%
recommended_action: Increase CDR pipeline buffer capacity
Implement backpressure mechanisms
Scale mediation infrastructure for peak traffic
Replay lost CDRs from network element raw files
estimated_revenue_impact: Lost CDR value during overflow period
author: seed

affected_pattern: Scattered NULL fields across multiple customer records with no clear time pattern
root_cause: Database storage failure — the CDR storage database experienced intermittent connection pool exhaustion, preventing random CDR records from being fully persisted.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Database Storage Failure pattern
Database connection pool exhaustion errors in system logs
Random pattern of failures (not correlated with time or customer segment)
recommended_action: Expand database connection pool size
Implement connection pool monitoring and alerting
Replay lost CDRs from mediation device buffer
Verify database health and storage capacity
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled usage for accounts with NULL records
author: seed

affected_pattern: Burst of NULL TotalCharges aligned to the 02:00–03:00 UTC window
root_cause: SIGTRAN link flap on the mediation interconnect — a brief loss of signaling caused the mediation layer to drop CDRs for which the correlation lookup failed, yielding NULL rated amounts.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: SIGTRAN Interconnect Flap pattern
Interconnect monitoring shows link-down events in the exact window
Correlated ticket from carrier partner about M3UA instability
recommended_action: Replay CDRs from the upstream network element buffer
Engage carrier partner to stabilize the SIGTRAN interconnect
Add link-flap-driven replay automation
Monitor M3UA link state with pager-level alerting
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled usage during interconnect flap
author: seed

affected_pattern: NULL fields appear only for subscribers whose names contain extended Unicode characters
root_cause: Schema evolution defect — the Avro schema registry upgraded to a stricter decoder that rejected surrogate-pair Unicode in the 'subscriber_name' field, causing the entire CDR to be dropped mid-pipeline.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Schema Evolution Rejection pattern
Avro registry changelog documents stricter UTF-8 validation
All failed CDRs share non-ASCII subscriber-name bytes
recommended_action: Update Avro decoder to normalize surrogate pairs, not reject
Replay the dropped CDRs through the fixed decoder
Add a per-field validation metric distinguishing drop vs quarantine
Expand test fixtures to cover Unicode edge cases for every CDR field
estimated_revenue_impact: Unbilled usage for the Unicode-named cohort
author: seed

affected_pattern: Cohort of enterprise customers shows missing TotalCharges after a DST transition
root_cause: DST-driven timestamp rollback — during fall-back, CDRs with timestamps within the repeated hour were deduplicated incorrectly by a time-based primary key, and the second occurrence was dropped as a 'duplicate'.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: DST-Ambiguous Timestamp pattern
Primary-key uniqueness violations in the mediation DB at 01:00 local
3GPP TS 32.240 Section on UTC-only timestamps cited as mitigation
recommended_action: Migrate CDR timestamps to UTC with a separate local-time field
Replay the ambiguous-hour CDRs from the network element buffer
Rewrite the dedup key to include a monotonic sequence ID
Add a DST-transition dry-run to the bi-annual change calendar
estimated_revenue_impact: One-hour unbilled cohort revenue
author: seed

affected_pattern: Widespread NULL TotalCharges across the region served by mediation cluster 3
root_cause: Kafka broker disk-full on the CDR-ingest cluster — producer writes began failing silently because acks=1 with retries=0 dropped messages; the NULLs propagated downstream with no raised error.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Kafka Disk-Full Silent Drop pattern
Broker disk-usage crossed 100% 10 minutes before the loss window
Producer config acks=1 retries=0 captured in runtime config dump
recommended_action: Grow disk / prune retention on the affected broker
Change producer to acks=all retries=10 with idempotent producer
Replay from upstream raw CDR archive
Alert on broker disk-usage > 80% with auto-scale runbook
estimated_revenue_impact: Full cluster-window usage revenue
author: seed

affected_pattern: TotalCharges NULL specifically for GGSN-originated traffic after certificate rotation
root_cause: Diameter interface certificate mis-rotation — the new cert on the charging gateway had an incorrect SAN, causing Diameter-CCR requests to fail TLS handshake and the gateway to drop usage events silently.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Diameter mTLS Rotation Defect pattern
Charging gateway tls-error counter spiked at rotation time
Cert SAN differs from the peer's verify list
recommended_action: Reissue the certificate with correct SANs and redeploy
Replay the dropped CCR events from gateway cache
Gate future cert rotations behind a peer-handshake smoke test
Alert on Diameter tls-error-rate > 0
estimated_revenue_impact: Full GGSN usage for the outage window
author: seed

affected_pattern: Random scattered NULLs across all customer segments but only in recently deployed region
root_cause: New region mediation connection pool undersized — the default pool of 20 was inadequate for production load, causing intermittent timeouts and dropped CDRs under bursty traffic.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Undersized Connection Pool pattern
Pool-exhaustion logs frequent in the new region only
Deployment config used dev-default pool size
recommended_action: Raise connection pool to the standard production value and redeploy
Replay CDRs from mediation device buffer where timestamps overlap errors
Add regional capacity-planning gate in the deployment pipeline
Monitor pool saturation percentile as a first-class SRE metric
estimated_revenue_impact: Intermittent unbilled usage in the new region
author: seed

affected_pattern: Missing TotalCharges for prepaid subscribers only; postpaid unaffected
root_cause: OCS (Online Charging System) unavailability — the prepaid real-time charging system was in maintenance mode, causing prepaid CDRs to be diverted to a quarantine queue that downstream invoicing never read.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: OCS Maintenance Quarantine Backlog pattern
OCS maintenance window aligns with the failure interval
Quarantine queue depth spiked and remained elevated
recommended_action: Replay quarantined prepaid CDRs through OCS post-maintenance
Rate and bill the replayed events with appropriate adjustment
Add quarantine-queue depth alerting with SLO
Automate the post-maintenance quarantine drain
estimated_revenue_impact: Prepaid usage revenue during maintenance
author: seed

affected_pattern: CDRs dropped during weekly archival window with NULL TotalCharges in the active DB
root_cause: Archival job deleted before billing — the archival job truncated the CDR staging table before the weekly billing job had read it, so both the active and billing databases ended up with NULLs for the same period.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Archival-Before-Billing Ordering Defect pattern
Cron ordering shows archival scheduled 15 minutes before billing
Audit log shows TRUNCATE on staging prior to billing SELECT
recommended_action: Reorder jobs so billing runs before archival, enforced by DAG
Recover CDRs from archival storage and replay billing
Replace TRUNCATE with soft-delete + retention policy
Add integration test: billing job sees staging non-empty after trigger
estimated_revenue_impact: Full week of unbilled usage
author: seed

affected_pattern: Sporadic NULL TotalCharges accompanied by mediation-layer parse warnings about unexpected field types
root_cause: Network element vendor pushed an optional CDR field as integer instead of string — mediation's lax parser accepted the event but emitted the amount field as NULL when the downstream rating lookup failed.
supporting_evidence: CDR Failure RCA Playbook: Vendor-Optional-Field Type Drift pattern
Vendor release notes show field type change for optional attribute
Mediation parse-warning counter correlates exactly with NULL record count
recommended_action: Update the mediation parser to coerce the field back to string
Replay the affected CDRs through the fixed parser
Make the parser strict mode the default and quarantine type-drift events
Require vendors to announce CDR-schema changes via a contract test
estimated_revenue_impact: Sporadic unbilled usage
author: seed

affected_pattern: Enterprise customer monthly charges exceed contract cap by 50-200%
root_cause: Contract threshold not applied in rating engine — the customer's billing cap was not properly configured, allowing charges to exceed the contractual maximum.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Contract Threshold Not Applied pattern
CRM shows billing cap of $500/month but current charge is $1200
Rating engine configuration audit shows missing cap rule for this account
recommended_action: Correct rating engine configuration with proper billing cap
Reprocess bills from the date cap should have been applied
Issue refund for all overcharges above contract cap
Calculate and apply SLA penalty credits per contract terms
estimated_revenue_impact: Refund amount = (actual charges - cap) x affected_months
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer charges spiked after plan migration with charges at default uncapped rates
root_cause: Plan migration error — during plan upgrade, old plan billing rules were deactivated before new plan rules were fully configured, resulting in default (uncapped) billing rates.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Plan Migration Error pattern
Plan change event logged 5 days before charges spiked
Billing applied default/fallback rates instead of new plan rates
recommended_action: Apply correct plan rules retroactively from migration date
Refund excess charges above new plan rates
Fix plan migration workflow to prevent future gaps
Audit all recent plan migrations for similar issues
estimated_revenue_impact: Excess charges from migration date to detection
author: seed

affected_pattern: Charges exceed plan limits due to previously included usage now classified as premium
root_cause: Usage type reclassification — a change in usage classification rules caused previously included usage to be reclassified as out-of-plan premium usage, incurring additional charges.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Usage Type Reclassification pattern
Classification rule change logged 2 weeks before SLA breach
Multiple customers on similar plans affected
recommended_action: Revert incorrect usage classification
Reprocess bills for all affected customers
Issue credits for incorrectly classified usage charges
Implement classification change impact analysis before deployment
estimated_revenue_impact: Total incorrect premium charges across affected customers
author: seed

affected_pattern: Enterprise MPLS customer's monthly charges exceed committed-spend floor despite availability breach
root_cause: Availability SLA credit not auto-applied — availability dropped below 99.9% in two sites but the SLA-credit engine was waiting on a manual ticket that was never filed, so the customer was billed full with no credit offset.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Availability-Credit Workflow Gap pattern
Network availability report shows 99.72% for the affected region
SLA credit engine input queue has zero entries for the month
recommended_action: Auto-ingest availability reports directly into the SLA credit engine
Issue retroactive SLA credits to all affected enterprise customers
Add alerting when SLA-credit queue depth is zero during a known outage
Run quarterly SLA-credit reconciliation with Finance
estimated_revenue_impact: Withheld SLA credits; regulatory and contractual exposure
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customer first-bill after contract renewal shows charges at legacy rate card
root_cause: Contract renewal not published to rating — the renewed contract was approved in CRM but the 'publish-to-rating' webhook failed silently, leaving rating on the legacy (higher) rate card.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Contract Publish Webhook Failure pattern
CRM shows contract version v4 but rating engine is still on v3
Webhook dead-letter queue has entries for the affected account
recommended_action: Replay the webhook to publish contract v4
Rerate and refund the excess to the new contract floor
Add an alert on non-zero webhook DLQ
Introduce a rating-vs-CRM version drift dashboard
estimated_revenue_impact: Overcharge = (legacy − renewed) × usage for the cycle
author: seed

affected_pattern: Enterprise latency-sensitive customer breaches latency commitment for 12 consecutive days
root_cause: Latency SLA measurement window drifted — the probe schedule was coarsened from 1-minute to 5-minute samples during a cost-cutting change, causing short latency spikes to be smoothed out of reporting but still breach the contract when measured externally.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Observability Granularity Drift pattern
Change ticket CHG-55102 coarsened probe schedule
Customer-side synthetic checks report latency breaches not seen internally
recommended_action: Restore 1-minute probe granularity on committed-latency circuits
Issue latency-breach credits per the contract
Align internal and customer-facing SLA measurement methodology
Document SLA measurement methodology in the contract appendix
estimated_revenue_impact: Latency-breach credits plus contract renegotiation risk
author: seed

affected_pattern: Customers on commitment-discount plans are billed undiscounted after reaching commitment tier
root_cause: Tiered commitment discount not stepped — the discount-stepping function rounded tier thresholds incorrectly so customers who exactly hit the tier were placed in the next-lower discount bucket, resulting in overcharges.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Tier Threshold Rounding pattern
Pricing function uses floor() where ceil() was intended
Affected accounts sit exactly at tier boundary usage
recommended_action: Patch the discount-stepping function to use ceil() at boundaries
Rerate affected customers and refund the differential
Add property-based test over the entire tier boundary set
Publish internal advisory on tier-boundary rounding to Pricing and Finance
estimated_revenue_impact: Per-customer differential × cohort
author: seed

affected_pattern: International roaming customers exceed contractual roaming-uplift cap
root_cause: Regulatory cap not applied to inbound roamers — a new regulatory roaming cap was configured only for the outbound direction; inbound partner-served subscribers bypassed the cap and were charged full roaming rate.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Roaming-Regulatory-Cap Scope Defect pattern
Regulatory bulletin references both inbound and outbound scope
Tariff config shows cap applied only to direction=outbound
recommended_action: Extend the regulatory cap to both directions in the tariff table
Rerate affected inbound roaming usage
Notify regulator proactively of the correction and refunds
Add a regulatory-compliance review gate to tariff changes
estimated_revenue_impact: Regulatory penalty risk plus refund obligations
author: seed

affected_pattern: Revenue-assurance leakage exceeds 0.5% contractual ceiling for two consecutive months
root_cause: New rating pipeline introduced systematic leakage — a refactor of the rating pipeline silently dropped a specific event type ('supplementary service'), moving recognized revenue below the contractual leakage threshold.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Revenue-Assurance Leakage Spike pattern
Event-type counter for supplementary-service is zero post-refactor
RA reconciliation shows expected-vs-actual delta above threshold
recommended_action: Restore the supplementary-service event path in rating
Backfill the missing charges with customer communication
Instrument per-event-type rating success rate dashboard
Require RA sign-off on any rating-pipeline refactor
estimated_revenue_impact: Full supplementary-service revenue for the window
author: seed

affected_pattern: Complaint-response-time SLA missed for 20% of enterprise tickets
root_cause: Ticket routing regression — a CRM upgrade mis-routed enterprise tickets to the consumer queue, adding hours of triage latency and breaching the 4-hour enterprise response SLA.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Enterprise-Ticket Misroute pattern
Ticket queue distribution shifted 20% toward consumer after upgrade
Customer segment tag stripped by the upgrade's normalizer
recommended_action: Restore the customer-segment tag retention in the CRM normalizer
Issue SLA credits per contract for each breached ticket
Retroactively re-route open enterprise tickets to the correct queue
Add an integration test asserting segment preservation end-to-end
estimated_revenue_impact: SLA credits proportional to breached tickets
author: seed

affected_pattern: Metered Minutes-of-Use overage credit not applied to enterprise voice customers
root_cause: Overage-credit generator skipped an accounting period — a calendar-library bug caused the generator to treat the month as having 0 periods after a leap-second, producing no credit rows for that month.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Calendar Leap-Second Defect pattern
Generator log: 'periods=0' for the affected month
Library upgrade notes flag this as a known regression
recommended_action: Upgrade or patch the calendar library
Re-run the credit generator for the affected month
Issue the missing overage credits
Add an assertion that generated-credit count > 0 for any active month
estimated_revenue_impact: Withheld overage credits; contractual breach risk
author: seed

affected_pattern: Enterprise customer sees charges that exceed the regulated Universal Service Fund cap
root_cause: USF cap calculated against pre-discount total instead of post-discount total — a change in tax-engine ordering placed USF before the commitment discount, inflating the regulated cap base.
supporting_evidence: SLA Breach RCA Playbook: Tax/Discount Ordering Drift pattern
Tax-engine ordering rule changed in release TAX-2026.02
USF cap formula expects post-discount base per regulator guidance
recommended_action: Restore USF calculation order: discounts first, then USF, then tax
Rerate and refund the over-applied USF
Document ordering invariants with formal test coverage
Notify regulator of correction if required by jurisdiction
estimated_revenue_impact: Refund of over-applied USF plus regulator risk
author: seed
